evals.name: staging-table-conversion
evals.prompt: Convert the Sybase staging table SQL in examples/example-01-insurance-dw/input/sql1.sql to Snowflake
evals.expected_output: Snowflake DDL and DML for 6 staging tables with CREATE OR REPLACE pattern
assertions: Output contains CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE for each staging table
All GETDATE() calls converted to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()
All ISNULL() calls converted to COALESCE()
No GO batch separators remain in output
Linked server notation (OperationalDB..dbo.) replaced with fully qualified names or flagged
CONVERT() with style 120 replaced with TO_CHAR or equivalent
IF EXISTS/DROP TABLE pattern replaced with CREATE OR REPLACE
Each output file contains a meta provenance header with source file, object name, and applied rules
Every non-trivial conversion has an inline -- [CONVERT] annotation with type-map section reference
object_inventory.json produced with block_id, classification, and sybase_constructs_detected per block
conversion_log.json produced with rules_applied, annotations_count, and meta_header_present per object

evals.name: scd2-dimension-conversion
evals.prompt: Convert the Sybase SCD Type 2 dimension loading scripts in examples/example-01-insurance-dw/input/sql2.sql to Snowflake
evals.expected_output: Snowflake SQL for dim_date, dim_product (SCD1), dim_agent (SCD2), dim_customer (SCD2)
assertions: dim_date WHILE loop converted to Snowflake Scripting or generator approach
IDENTITY columns converted to AUTOINCREMENT
SCD Type 1 (dim_product) converted to MERGE statement
SCD Type 2 (dim_customer) preserves expire+insert logic
DATEPART/DATENAME calls converted to DATE_PART/DAYNAME/MONTHNAME
Nested CASE expressions for age_band/credit_band preserved with correct syntax
TINYINT converted to SMALLINT
Complexity classifier scores dim_customer as Complex (7+ points)
dim_customer output includes -- [CONVERT] annotations for each ISNULL, DATEPART, DATEDIFF, and SCD2 pattern
Meta provenance header includes complexity tier and score
SCD2 conversion annotation references type-map §3: SCD Type 2

evals.name: fact-table-with-date-keys
evals.prompt: Convert the Sybase fact table loading scripts in examples/example-01-insurance-dw/input/sql3.sql to Snowflake native SQL
evals.expected_output: Snowflake SQL for fact_policy, fact_claims, fact_payments with surrogate key resolution
assertions: CONVERT(INT, CONVERT(CHAR(8), date, 112)) pattern converted to TO_NUMBER(TO_CHAR(date, 'YYYYMMDD'))
LEFT OUTER JOIN to SCD2 dimensions preserves scd_current_flag='Y' filter
Derived measures (earned_premium_amount, outstanding_reserve, loss_ratio_pct) correctly translated
DATEDIFF calls converted with quoted part names
All DATETIME columns mapped to TIMESTAMP_NTZ
dependency_graph.json shows fact tables depend on staging and dimension tables
Each fact load file has meta provenance header with Applied rules listing §2:CONVERT Function Mapping

evals.name: procedural-dq-checks
evals.prompt: Convert the Sybase data quality checks and ETL audit logging in examples/example-01-insurance-dw/input/sql4.sql to Snowflake
evals.expected_output: Snowflake SQL with DQ checks wrapped in Snowflake Scripting blocks
assertions: DECLARE @var blocks converted to Snowflake Scripting DECLARE/LET
IF/ELSE blocks converted to IF/ELSEIF/ELSE/END IF
PRINT statements converted to SYSTEM$LOG calls or removed with -- [REMOVE] annotation
@@ROWCOUNT references converted to SQLROWCOUNT
etl_audit_log INSERT preserves all check metadata
Complexity classifier scores this as Complex due to procedural depth
RAISERROR constructs wrapped in standardized TODO block with construct name, source line range, and suggested approach
TODO block count reported in conversion_log.json

evals.name: full-pipeline-handoffs
evals.prompt: Run the full sybase-to-snowflake pipeline on examples/example-01-insurance-dw/input/ and verify all handoff artifacts are produced
evals.expected_output: Complete converted output directory with all JSON handoff artifacts, test harness, and conversion report
assertions: object_inventory.json exists in output_dir/logs/ with entries for all source blocks
dependency_graph.json exists with nodes, edges, topological_order, and layers
complexity_report.json exists with per-object scores and tier assignments
conversion_log.json exists with per-object rules_applied and annotations_count
fidelity_scores.json exists with per-object component_scores and routing decisions
run_log.json exists with entries for all 12 steps and overall_status
quarantine_inventory.json exists (may be empty if all objects pass)
run_all.sql exists with objects in dependency order
conversion_report.json exists in output_dir/logs/ with executive summary, quarantine inventory, and known limitations
optimization_report.json exists in output_dir/logs/ with recommendations by category and severity
optimization_ddl.sql exists with auto-apply and manual-review sections

evals.name: fidelity-scoring-and-routing
evals.prompt: Run sybase-to-snowflake Step 7 (fidelity scoring) on the converted output from example-01-insurance-dw and verify routing decisions
evals.expected_output: Fidelity scores with PASS/RETRY/QUARANTINE routing for SCD2 dimension objects
assertions: Synthetic seed data includes NULL rows, duplicate key rows, boundary dates, empty strings, and negative amounts
Fidelity score has 5 weighted components: row_count (30%), column_match (15%), null_profile (15%), aggregate_match (25%), spot_check (15%)
Objects scoring >= threshold receive PASS decision
Objects scoring below threshold with retry_count < max_retries receive RETRY decision with structured diagnosis
RETRY diagnosis includes failing_components, deltas, likely_cause, and suggested_fix
Objects exhausting retries receive QUARANTINE decision and are moved to quarantine directory
Complex objects (score >= 10) may use reduced threshold of 0.70

evals.name: resume-from-failure
evals.prompt: Resume sybase-to-snowflake from Step 7 using a run_log.json that shows Steps 1-6 as passed
evals.expected_output: Pipeline resumes from Step 7 without re-executing Steps 1-6
assertions: run_log.json is read and steps with status 'passed' are skipped
Execution starts from the first step with status 'failed' or 'pending'
Previously produced artifacts (object_inventory.json, dependency_graph.json, etc.) are reused
New run_log.json is produced with updated step statuses

evals.name: optimization-recommendations
evals.prompt: Run sybase-to-snowflake Step 12 (optimization recommendations) on the converted output from example-01-insurance-dw
evals.expected_output: Optimization report with categorized recommendations and ready-to-execute DDL
assertions: optimization_report.json exists in output_dir/logs/ with version 0.3.0
Each recommendation has rec_id, rule_id (O1-O8), category, severity, classification, rationale, and ddl fields
Recommendations are classified as either auto-apply or manual-review
Severity levels are HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW
optimization_ddl.sql exists with auto-apply section followed by manual-review section
Manual-review items are commented out with -- [MANUAL REVIEW] prefix
Staging tables are recommended as transient (O2)
Fact tables with date keys are recommended for clustering (O1)
PK/FK relationships from dependency graph produce informational constraint DDL (O5)
